rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MILTON WISE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/24/20. Original bank statement must be filed. [LRSF 14.62.] A declaration must be filed re the retention of original documents. [LRSF 2.11 M.4.] A copy of the seller's closing statement must be filed to verify the costs of sale. Payment for Document Preparation Services must reduce the statutory commission. the filing fee paid on 9/6/18 was $435, not $450. The will at Article V, paragraph (c) named Marsha Lynnae Drew (not Landers) and Gwendolyn R. Wise (not Davis) among the beneficiaries of 90%. No 13100 affidavits have been filed for the successors-in-interest to Arthur Rur Wise and Gwendolyn R. Davis. If these issues cannot be addressed in time to deliver courtesy copies of all filed papers to the assigned Examiner by 11/13/20, the matter will likely be continued again. Additional comments: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] The first page of each filed paper must include the name, office address, telephone number, fax number and e-mail address (if available), and State Bar membership number of the attorney for the party in whose behalf the paper is presented. [CRC 2.111.] The identification must show the name and capacity of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, not just "Petitioner." [LRSF 14.17.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ERNEST V. RHINE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Kimberly Rhine Gagosian?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Withdraw Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Respondent's Motion to Withdraw Admission to Request for Admissions, Set One, Nos. 9 and 10 is granted under CCP section 2030.300. The Court finds that these admissions were the result of Respondent's mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect in not carefully examining the notary's certificates and incorrectly concluding that Respondent's undated signature was executed on the same day as the other, dated signatures on the amended trust document. The Court finds that Petitioner will not be substantially prejudiced by the withdrawal of these two admissions in maintaining the undue influence claim. Although an admission or denial regarding who was or was not in the room when a challenged instrument was signed is potentially relevant, it is not dispositive of an undue influence claim. "What constitutes undue influence and what constitutes sufficient proof thereof depend upon the facts and circumstances of each particular case. It 'is a species of constructive fraud which the courts will not undertake to define by any fixed principles, lest the very definition itself furnish a finger-board pointing out the path by which it may be evaded.'" (Sparks v. Sparks (1950) 101 Cal. App. 2d 129, 135, quoting Longmire v. Kruger (1926) 80 Cal.App. 230, 239.) Further, it appears that discovery is ongoing in this matter, and that facts regarding the circumstances under which the challenged instrument was signed can be gathered through further discovery. Pursuant to CCP section 2033.300(c)(1), the Court further orders that Petitioner shall be permitted to pursue additional discovery reasonably related to the matter involved in the withdrawn admissions, specifically whether either Respondent or her husband, or both of them, was present when the amended trust instrument was signed on March 19, 2005. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on November 3, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JO ANN YEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/02/2020 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) The beginning date of the account must be the date of death. LRSF 14.59. 2) Why is the ending date of the account 01/31/2020, even though the petition was filed on 07/06/2020? 3) Paragraph 10: Have 2019 income tax returns been filed? 4) Paragraph 12B: The date of liquidations is different from the dates set forth on Schedule E. 5) Paragraph 12F: The Cathy Bank account number is incorrectly stated. 6) Paragraph 20: The Court's record does not show the payment of $25.50 for a certified copy of letters. Please provide a copy of the receipt or other proof of this payment. 7) Paragraph 24b: An allegation as to how unknown or newly discovered property should be distributed must be added. 8) Assets on Hand Schedule: The content of the Neuberger Berman account is incorrectly described. 9) Schedule A: Dates of payments of interest and other income from Morgan Stanley are not set forth. 10) Schedule A shows payments from Medical Properties Trust which does not appear on the inventory and appraisal or other Schedules. 11) Schedule A shows some receipts from Principal Short Term and distributions from Medical Properties Trust and Sun Communities prior to the date of death. 12) Schedule D: Are the payments to Santos Hauling on 01/08/2019 and Airtech Solutions on 0827/2019 rental expenses attributable to the Cabrillo property? Should they be reported on Schedule C? 13) Are any taxes on the rental income reported on Schedule D? 14) Schedule D: No dates are set forth for two payments of advisory fees to Morgan Stanley. Also, Schedule D shows the first advisory fee of $590.36 paid to Morgan Stanley prior to the date of death. 15) Schedule E: How did the petitioners determine the carry value ($494.63) of 41.081 shares of Principal Short Term sold on 11/05/2018? 16) A declaration regarding the retention of the original Fremont Bank account statement must be filed. LRSF 14.93D. 17) Reimbursement Schedule: Was the purpose of the $1,000 retainer paid on 07/05/2018 to cover filing fees and other costs incurred by the petitioners' attorney? If so, why is the attorney requesting reimbursement of $1,085.50? 18) No creditor claim for funeral expenses is on file, nor has the claim been approved by the Court. Probate Code sections 9000(a)(3) & 9252. See also LRSF 14.49B. 19) Please explain why the estate funds exceeding the FDIC limit have been kept in a single account. If the petitioners have opened an additional account at a different institution to deposit the excess funds, please provide proof of the deposit. 20) The Fremont Bank account statement shows the payments of $19.04 on 01/09/2020 and $90.87 on 01/28/2020, but Schedule D does not.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JIA LIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although paragraph 15 of the petition is untrue as most of the estate was cash and there was no real property, and the assets on hand are reportedly exactly the same as on the date of death, which is not reflective of prudent estate management. Paragraph 22 II(a)of the petition also contains an erroneous statement. Probate Code Section 9202(b) also requires notice if a beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. The order appointing the executor was signed on 11/27/19 but not filed until 12/2/19.
